title: genista tinctoria subsp. ovata
intro: Genista tinctoria subsp. ovata, also known as dyer's greenweed, is a perennial plant species native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as skin conditions, respiratory problems, and digestive issues, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and promoting wound healing. Dyers greenweed can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used as a dye plant for natural textiles.
